Pancratium is a genus of plants in the family Amaryllidaceae, commonly known as the lily family. These plants are flowering bulbs that typically grow in dry, sandy areas and have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits.

The health benefits of plants in the Pancratium genus are thought to include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and combat infections.

The bioactive constituents of Pancratium plants include alkaloids, such as pancratistatin and homopancratistatin, which have been shown to have anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Pancratium plants are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and infections.
